Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

could you post an obviously flawed interpretation of the philosophy heard in class at a temple?

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2008-06-09 · 9 answers
please omit devotee name to prevent embarrassment and possible breach of etiquette :)
I start with this one:

______________
devotee in SBhagavatam class:
"Lets face it, we hate Krishna!"
_______________

no comments
user [149] · 2008-06-09
Devotee giving SB class quoting a Swami:

"Krishna engages us in sense gratification."
user [19] · 2008-06-09
SB CLASS
"The material world is not real"
user [154] · 2008-06-09
I have to be very honest. As a survival technique I have developed an ability not to remember all and stopped going for the classed of people I knew who would stay things like that. I think there should be more reading of selections of quotes for those characters, rather then long stories about things they do not understand.

I do remember intelligent things I do not agree with. For example:

@KC is a social thing. We ether all go back to godhead or all stay in this material world.
user [149] · 2008-06-09
"Lord Caitanya doesnt exist in the spiritual world."
user [24] · 2008-06-09
[quote][cite] deena:[/cite]"Lord Caitanya doesnt exist in the spiritual world."[/quote]

Thats a horrible thought.
user [24] · 2008-06-09
From ISKCON Sydney vyasasana:

"Even if you go out on book distribution without the consent of the temple authorities its just mundane activity!"
